message:
fix(config,legacy): prioritize portable ~/.config path over native platform dirs

Refactors env file discovery in both the CLI legacy module and the
core config module to prefer the portable installer path at
~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env over platform-native config
directories (e.g. ~/Library/Application Support/ on macOS). This
ensures consistent behavior across platforms and aligns with the
curl|bash installer which always writes to ~/.config/.

Key changes:
- core/config.rs: user_env_file_candidates() now checks ~/.config/...
  first, then falls back to native XDG/platform dir, then legacy paths.
  Deduplicates candidates when portable and native dirs are the same.
- cli/legacy.rs: discover_user_env_file() refactored into a testable
  discover_user_env_file_from() that accepts home and native config dir,
  with the same portable-first priority order.
- core/legacy.rs: new file adding legacy detection capabilities to the
  core crate for reuse outside the CLI.
- Added tests verifying portable path wins over native on macOS and
  that candidate lists contain no duplicates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
